title: Virtual Private Network (VPN)
description: Encrypted tunnel that protects data in transit and hides your IP address.
fullExplanation: A VPN creates an encrypted tunnel between your device and a VPN server. All your internet traffic flows through this tunnel, protecting it from eavesdropping. Your ISP, network admin, and attackers on the same network cannot see what you're doing. The destination website sees the VPN server's IP, not yours. VPNs are essential for remote work, public WiFi, and bypassing censorship.

types.name: Remote Access VPN
types.description: Individual users connect to corporate network from home/travel

types.name: Site-to-Site VPN
types.description: Connect two entire networks (e.g., branch offices)

types.name: SSL/TLS VPN
types.description: Browser-based VPN, no client software needed

types.name: Consumer VPN
types.description: Commercial VPN services for privacy and geo-unblocking

protocols.name: WireGuard
protocols.description: Modern, fast, simple codebase, built into Linux kernel

protocols.name: L2TP/IPsec
protocols.description: Combines L2TP tunneling with IPsec encryption

protocols.name: PPTP
protocols.description: Old, fast, but insecure — do not use

keyFacts: VPN encrypts all traffic, not just HTTPS
Protects against MITM attacks on public WiFi
VPN provider can see your traffic — choose trustworthy provider
WireGuard is faster than OpenVPN (modern cryptography)
Split tunneling routes some traffic through VPN, some direct
Kill switch blocks internet if VPN disconnects
benefits: Secures remote access to corporate network
limitations: VPN provider can see your traffic
Not anonymous (VPN knows your real IP)
Free VPNs often log and sell data
Does not protect against malware or phishing
